message:
fix(policy, runtime): self-gc RateLimiter + unblock non-native-wezterm build (ft-pp7jk follow-up)

Two independent follow-ups to ft-pp7jk (closed in d37d3965 via the
handle_lifecycle_event adapter at policy.rs:5082).

1. policy::RateLimiter self-gc (defense-in-depth)

The ft-pp7jk adapter makes the wiring a one-liner for any caller that
subscribes to Event::PaneDisappeared. This adds a complementary bound
that works even WITHOUT a subscriber: when `pane_counts` exceeds a soft
cap (PANE_COUNTS_AUTO_GC_THRESHOLD = 1024), check() runs gc_at(now)
inline to prune stale timestamps. Stale (pane_id, action) entries age
out automatically once their timestamps pass `window`.

This closes the original ft-yjt9e concern (unbounded pane_counts growth
in long-lived PolicyEngine holders) even if a future caller forgets to
wire handle_lifecycle_event.

Two regression tests:
  - rate_limiter_self_gc_bounds_pane_counts_under_churn: 2000-pane
    churn with 1ms window + 5μs sleep between iterations must leave
    pane_counts strictly < 2000 (pre-fix would stay at 2000).
  - rate_limiter_self_gc_preserves_active_entries_across_threshold:
    1500 panes within a 1h window all stay tracked (no false eviction).

2. runtime::ft_pp7jk_* tests — feature-gate + recv_cx fix

The two runtime tests landed in 41cab3bb reference `handle_native_event`
(gated on `feature = "native-wezterm"`) and call `subscriber.recv(&cx)`,
but:
  - Under `--no-default-features --features asupersync-runtime`, the
    function doesn't exist → E0425.
  - `EventBus::Subscriber::recv(&mut self)` takes 0 args; passing &cx
    matches `recv_cx` → E0061.

Add `#[cfg(feature = "native-wezterm")]` to both tests and rename the
call to `recv_cx(&cx)`. Tests now compile under the minimal feature
set the CI runs on (and still run under the default set which includes
native-wezterm).

18 policy + runtime tests pass (rate_limiter family under asupersync-runtime).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
